title: 10 best herbal teas for migraine

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like parthenolide, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms by blocking the production of pain-promoting chemicals in the brain.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The active compounds in feverfew tea, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, can help constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce the throbbing sensation and pain associated with migraines.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew tea is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and migraine development. By reducing oxidative stress, feverfew tea may help prevent or alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, teas helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to inhibit the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in migraine pain. The tea's active compounds, such as parthenolide, have been shown to block the release of pain-causing chemicals, including prostaglandins and histamine. Additionally, feverfew tea may help reduce blood vessel constriction, a common cause of migraine headaches. Regular consumption of feverfew tea may provide relief from migraine symptoms and frequency.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: A sesquiterpene lactone, parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and anti-migraine properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Tanacetol: A phenolic compound, tanacetol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Camphor: A terpene, camphor has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving pain and reducing inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Tanacetum parthenium are commonly used to make teas for migraines due to their high concentration of parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers are also frequently used to make teas, as they are rich in parthenolide and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce migraine severity and frequency.

Stems: The stems of Tanacetum parthenium are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain a smaller amount of parthenolide compared to the leaves and flowers, but still offer some therapeutic benefits for migraines.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers from a reputable source.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or kettle for 2-3 minutes.
Steep the dried flowers in the heated water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors and oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consumption within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Salicin: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound, salicin helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines by blocking the production of prostaglandins.

Flavonoids: White willow tea contains flavonoids like quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, potentially reducing migraine frequency and severity.
Tannins: The tannins present in white willow tea act as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce pain associated with migraines.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, teas helps with migraine because of its unique combination of compounds that provide natural pain relief. The tea contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory that works similarly to aspirin to reduce pain and inflammation in the body. Additionally, Salix alba has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches and sensitivity to light and sound. Regular consumption may help to reduce migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A glycoside that helps with migraine by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with migraine attacks.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): A type of phenolic compound that helps with migraine by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to migraine development.

Salicylic acid: A phenolic acid that helps with migraine by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with migraine attacks, as well as helping to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve migraine pain and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Buds: The buds are used due to their ability to release astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to constrict blood vessels and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather salix alba leaves and twigs from a reliable source and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried salix alba leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or let it cool to enjoy as a warm or cold beverage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a common migraine trigger, thereby alleviating migraine symptoms.
Pain-relieving properties: The bioactive compounds in ginger tea, like gingerol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help relieve migraine pain, nausea, and discomfort.

Digestive soothing properties: Ginger tea's carminative properties help to reduce stomach upset, nausea, and digestive discomfort associated with migraines, which can be a major trigger for some people.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with migraine because its active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds work by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals, reducing inflammation, and alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with migraines. Additionally, ginger's ability to relax muscles and improve blood circulation may help to alleviate migraine symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking relief from this debilitating condition. Regular consumption of ginger tea may provide long-term benefits.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid compounds, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the body.

Shogaols: The shogaols present in Zingiber officinale, especially [6]-shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce migraine severity and frequency by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Zingerone: This compound, a decomposition product of gingerols, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with migraines, thereby providing relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the most commonly used parts of Zingiber officinale for tea due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale are also used in teas to help alleviate migraine symptoms, as they contain volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale, rich in essential oils, are used in teas to help reduce migraine pain and inflammation due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root for every 8 oz of water needed.
Steep the zingiber officinale root in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the root and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired for a sweeter flavor.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Pain relief: Peppermint's menthol content helps to numb the pain receptors, providing fast-acting relief from migraine headache symptoms, such as throbbing or pulsating pain.

Vasodilation and relaxation: The menthol and camphor in peppermint tea help to dilate blood vessels and relax muscle tissue, which can alleviate migraines caused by vasoconstriction and muscle tension.

Digestive aid: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, such as IBS, that are commonly linked to migraines, reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the brain and relieve tension. The menthol content in peppermint tea relaxes the muscles, eases pressure in the temples, and calms the mind. This natural remedy also has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce the intensity of migraine symptoms. By promoting blood flow and reducing inflammation, peppermint tea provides a natural and effective way to alleviate migraine discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol, a terpene compound, helps with migraine by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the brain.
Menthone: Menthone, another terpene compound, helps with migraine by inhibiting the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters, such as substance P, which play a role in migraine pain.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, helps with migraine by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are contributing factors to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide relief from migraine symptoms.
Buds: The buds are utilized for their high menthol content, which helps to ease migraine pain and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly used, contain some menthol and menthone, making them a suitable alternative when leaves and buds are not available.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from the plant in the early morning or late afternoon when the temperature is mild.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea and serve immediately or let it cool before refrigerating for later consumption.

remedy_properties: Calming effects: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Pain relief: Lavender contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate headache pain and reduce inflammation associated with migraines.

Anti-nausea effects: Lavender's essential oils have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting in patients, which is a common symptom of migraines, by acting on the brain's vomiting center and reducing serotonin levels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with migraine because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, thereby alleviating migraine symptoms. Lavender tea also promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, which are common triggers for migraines. The tea's soothing effects can help alleviate migraine pain and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular natural remedy for those affected by this condition. Regular consumption may provide relief and reduce frequency.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve muscle spasms associated with migraines.

Linalyl acetate: Another terpene present in lavender tea, linalyl acetate has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that often trigger migraines.

Lavandulol: A phenolic compound found in lavender tea, lavandulol has been demonstrated to have anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which help relax muscles and ease migraine pain.
Flowers: Contain calming properties due to the presence of linalool and other volatile oils, which can help reduce migraine symptoms.
Stems: May be used in some herbal teas, although their usage is less common compared to leaves and flowers, and may still offer some benefits due to their essential oil content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer for 1 to 2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water for a standard tea preparation.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes and then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve.
Store the cooled lavandula angustifolia tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing effects: Valerian tea helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress, which can trigger or worsen migraine symptoms.
Pain relief and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's sesquiterpenes have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it effective in easing migraine headaches.

GABA-enhancing and antispasmodic effects: Valerian increases GABA levels in the brain, which helps regulate muscle spasms and tension that can contribute to migraines, while its antispasmodic properties help relax blood vessels and reduce headache pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with migraine because of its natural relaxant and pain-relieving properties. The roots of the valerian plant contain valerenic acid, which has been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, valerian teas can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches and sensitivity to light and sound. Regular consumption of valerian teas may also help reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.
Valeranone: Valeranone, a valerenic acid derivative, has been found to have a sedative effect and can help reduce muscle spasms, which are often associated with migraines.

Valerenol: Valerenol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, has been demonstrated to have a neuroprotective effect and can help prevent the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters, such as glutamate, which are involved in migraine pathophysiology.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and other active compounds that have a sedative effect and help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: They are used due to their lower concentration of active compounds compared to the roots and rhizomes, but are still beneficial for making teas for migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Dry the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks or until they crumble easily.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea immediately and discard the solids to prevent bitterness and sediment formation.

remedy_properties: Pain-relieving flavonoids: Maypop tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it effective in mitigating migraine symptoms.
Antioxidant-rich anthocyanins: The high concentration of anthocyanins in Maypop tea helps to neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to migraines.

GABA-boosting alkaloids: Maypop tea contains alkaloids like pasiflorine, which have been shown to increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate pain perception and relaxation responses, potentially reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, teas helps with migraine because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and alkaloids. These compounds interact with brain chemistry to produce a calming effect, helping to reduce migraine frequency and severity. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Passiflora incarnata teas can alleviate migraine symptoms, such as pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light. Its natural sedative properties also help regulate sleep patterns, contributing to overall migraine management and relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity, which contributes to their analgesic and anti-anxiety effects.
Harman: This indole alkaloid has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce migraine frequency and severity by regulating sleep patterns and stress response.
Isovitexin: This flavonoid glycoside is known for its vasodilatory properties, which may help relieve migraine headaches by reducing blood vessel constriction and promoting blood flow to the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on migraine relief.
Fruits: Fruits are also widely used as they contain a high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help in reducing migraine symptoms.
Roots: Roots are another popular choice for their high content of passifloric acid, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help in alleviating migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from the plant in late summer or early fall.
Crush 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves into a fine powder.
Steep the powdered passiflora incarnata in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before serving and consuming within 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and nerves, which can contribute to migraine pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety, which is often a trigger for migraines, and promote relaxation to ease migraine symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms in blood vessels and nerves can help relieve tension headaches and migraines caused by vasospasm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with migraine because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that interacts with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of apigenin also help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Matricaria chamomilla teas can provide relief from migraine symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relax muscles and reduce pain associated with migraines.
Furanoflavonoids (e.g., matricarin): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and inflammation.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Matricaria chamomilla to make teas for migraines due to their high concentration of apigenin, an antispasmodic compound that helps alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them a popular choice for soothing migraine pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for migraines due to their high oil content, which contains sesquiterpene lactones that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 1 cup of boiling water for the tea.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids from the liquid.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired to sweeten the herbal tea.
Drink the warm tea immediately to experience the calming effects of matricaria chamomilla.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties can help to relax tense muscles, reduce spasms, and ease migraine pain by calming the nervous system.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common triggers of migraines.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help to alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of tension.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with migraine because of its natural calming and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol, have been shown to ease tension and reduce inflammation in the brain, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, the tea's sedative effects can help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted during migraine episodes. As a result, Melissa officinalis tea may provide relief from migraine symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent migraine attacks by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing inflammation in the brain.
Melissic Acid: A triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Flowers: They are often used in teas due to their calming effects and ability to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers.

Stems: While not as widely used as leaves or flowers, stems of Melissa officinalis are sometimes used in teas for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce migraine severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for the best flavor.
Dry the fresh melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids after 2 to 3 minutes.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving over ice or chilled.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are thought to contribute to the development and severity of migraines.

Vasodilatory effects: The terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba tea can help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, which may help to relieve migraine symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoid compounds have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation in the brain and blood vessels that can contribute to migraines.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with migraine because of its ability to improve blood flow to the brain. This increased circulation is believed to help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the brain. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba may also help to reduce pain and sensitivity associated with migraines. Additionally, it may help to relax blood vessels and reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Biflavones and Flavone Glycosides): These compounds help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and preventing platelet aggregation, which may contribute to migraine attacks.

Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce migraine severity by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as serotonin, and blocking the action of platelet-activating factor, which is involved in migraine pathophysiology.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine development and severity, and also has a protective effect on neuronal cells, potentially alleviating migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Contain bilobalide, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce migraine severity.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-6 high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves completely in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to preserve their potency.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb grinder.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped ginkgo biloba leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving the tea hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with migraine because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, particularly hyperforin and flavonoids. These ingredients have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can effectively reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. This can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Additionally, St John's Wort has a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing tension that may contribute to migraines.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache pain and inflammation.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to migraine development.
Hypericum oil: The oil extracted from St. John's Wort flowers has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of St John's Wort have been traditionally used to ease migraine pain and inflammation.

Flowers: With their high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, flowers of St John's Wort are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective for migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers from a trusted source or store.
Measure out two teaspoons of the dried herb and place it in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Boil fresh water and pour one cup over the herb, letting it steep for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, taking up to three cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw teas helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling in the blood vessels that can trigger migraines. The herbal tea also contains harpagoside, a compound that has been shown to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, devil's claw teas may help regulate serotonin levels, which is thought to contribute to migraine development. By sipping on this soothing tea, individuals may find relief from the debilitating symptoms of migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Devil's claw tea has been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and swelling in the head.

Pain-relieving properties: The active compounds in devil's claw tea, including harpagoside and cimiracemoside, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a natural remedy for relieving headache and migraine pain.

Antispasmodic properties: Devil's claw tea has been shown to relax muscle spasms and reduce tension in the head and neck, which can contribute to migraines by helping to prevent blood vessels from constricting and causing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, have been traditionally used to ease headaches and tension due to their sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried harpagophytum procumbens roots.
Combine the root powder with one cup of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to dissolve the sweetener.
Let the tea steep for an additional five minutes before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with migraine because of its unique compound curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a tea, curcumin can help to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is often a contributing factor to migraines. Additionally, turmeric's natural pain-relieving properties may help to alleviate symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound. By reducing inflammation and providing pain relief, herbal turmeric teas offer a soothing and natural remedy for migraine sufferers.
remedy_properties: Curcumin content: Turmeric tea contains high levels of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce migraine frequency and severity by blocking pain pathways in the brain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric tea help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which are thought to play a role in migraines.

GABA-boosting effects: Turmeric contains compounds that have been shown to increase levels of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate pain perception and can help reduce migraine frequency by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of turmeric contains a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in relieving migraine symptoms.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including migraines, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried turmeric root and 1 teaspoon of dried ginger powder for each serving.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or saucepan for at least 10 minutes.
Steep the turmeric root and ginger powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it within 30 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng teas helps with migraine because it has been traditionally used to enhance energy, reduce stress, and improve circulation. The tea's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to changing conditions, which can help alleviate symptoms of migraines such as fatigue, anxiety, and sensitivity to light and sound. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the brain, a common trigger for migraine attacks. By promoting overall well-being and reducing stress, Siberian ginseng tea may provide relief from migraine headaches.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Siberian Ginseng tea helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers, by adapting to changing circumstances and promoting emotional balance.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Siberian Ginseng tea help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to migraines.

Vasodilatory properties: The tea's ability to dilate blood vessels may help to alleviate migraine symptoms by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing constriction of blood vessels, which is a common migraine cause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, Siberian ginseng roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Rhizomes: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, Siberian ginseng rhizomes are believed to possess adaptogenic properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety triggers for migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat filtered water to a boil and then let it cool for 3-5 minutes before pouring over the herb.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in order to allow the active compounds to infuse fully.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup to remove any solids.
Enjoy the tea hot or let it cool to room temperature and drink within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha teas helps with migraine because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger migraines. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to regulate cortisol levels, which can become imbalanced and exacerbate migraine symptoms. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines. By sipping on a warm cup of ashwagandha tea, individuals may find relief from the debilitating symptoms of migraines, allowing them to feel more balanced and relaxed throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help regulate stress hormones, which is a common trigger for migraines. By reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, ashwagandha may help prevent migraine attacks.

Pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha contains compounds that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain, a common cause of migraines.

Antioxidant properties: Ashwagandha's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to migraines. By neutralizing free radicals and reducing cell damage, ashwagandha may help prevent migraines or reduce their frequency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ashwagandha roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like withanolides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and anxiety, both common triggers of migraines.
Leaves: Ashwagandha leaves possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them an effective addition to teas for migraine relief by reducing pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried ashwagandha roots in cold water.
Steep the ashwagandha roots in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain and discard solids from the tea using a fine-mesh strainer.
Combine the resulting tea with equal parts of fresh ginger juice.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for an additional 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort teas helps with migraine because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that work together to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax tense muscles and blood vessels, which can contribute to migraines. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort's ability to calm the nervous system can help alleviate symptoms such as sensitivity to light and sound, making it a natural remedy for migraine sufferers seeking relief from debilitating headaches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort tea's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to migraine development.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Asiatic Pennywort tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing swelling and pain in blood vessels and nerve tissue.

Vasodilatory properties: Asiatic Pennywort tea's vasodilatory effects can help relax constricted blood vessels, which is a common cause of migraines, and improve blood flow to the brain, reducing migraine severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Asiatic pennywort plant are rich in alkaloids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to relieve pain and tension associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers for maximum potency.
Rinse 1-2 tablespoons of dried or 3-4 tablespoons of fresh centella asiatica with filtered water.
Boil 250ml of water in a pot and steep the herb mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids.
Drink the warm or cooled tea immediately after preparation, ideally before migraine onset.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with migraine because it has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels. The herb's natural antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to migraine pain. Additionally, rosemary's ability to relax and soothe muscle tension in the neck and scalp may help reduce migraine-related discomfort and stiffness. Overall, herbal rosemary tea is a natural and effective way to alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are known migraine triggers.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in rosemary tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce blood vessel dilation and relieve migraine pain.
Vasodilatory properties: Rosemary's camphor content causes blood vessels to dilate, which can help to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce migraine symptoms such as headaches and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help reduce inflammation and pain, making them an effective component in teas for migraine relief.
Barks: Rosemary barks contain compounds like camphor and bornyl acetate, which are known to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes in order to extract the essential oils and compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it, taking one cup three times daily as needed.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops teas helps with migraine because of its unique composition that targets the underlying causes of this debilitating condition. The flavonoids present in hops, such as xanthohumol, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are known triggers for migraines. Additionally, the sedative effects of hops can help to relax tense muscles and calm the mind, reducing symptoms like headache pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea associated with migraine attacks.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Hops contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.

Pain-Relieving and Inflammation-Reducing Properties: Hops have been found to possess analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headache pain and reduce inflammation that may contribute to migraines.

Anti-Seizure and Neuroprotective Properties: Hops have been shown to possess anticonvulsant and neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or reduce the frequency of migraines by reducing excitotoxicity and oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Buds: Hops buds are rich in volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and may help reduce migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers ensuring adequate sunlight exposure for optimal potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried humulus lupulus material with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes allowing the flavonoids and xanthohumol to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea leaves and flowers using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids before consumption.
Enjoy the tea hot or iced within one hour of preparation consuming a full cup three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with migraine because of its unique ability to calm inflammation in the body. The plant's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and 6-caffeoylsalicilic acid, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are hormone-like substances that can trigger headache pain. Additionally, dandelion teas may also help to relax blood vessels in the brain, reducing blood flow and pressure that can contribute to migraine symptoms. By targeting these underlying causes, dandelion tea has been found to be a natural and effective way to alleviate migraine pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce inflammation in the brain and alleviate migraine symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, such as vitamin C and flavonoids, can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to migraine onset and progression.

Vasodilation and diuretic effects: Dandelion tea's vasodilating properties can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which may reduce migraine pain by reducing vascular constriction; its diuretic effects can also help eliminate excess fluid and electrolytes that may exacerbate migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine pain.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to relax blood vessels and reduce the frequency of migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale roots or leaves per cup.
Heat filtered water in a kettle or pot until it reaches a simmering temperature.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly and drink immediately after preparation.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal teas are tension headaches, cluster headaches, and fibromyalgia. Herbal teas such as feverfew, lavender, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate symptoms like headache pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and muscle stiffness. These teas may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, common triggers for these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for migraine are natural remedies that help alleviate migraine symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and soothing the mind and body. The best herbal teas for migraine are Foeniculum vulgare, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Passiflora incarnata, Achillea millefolium, Cinnamomum verum, and Matricaria chamomilla. If left untreated, migraines can severely impact daily life, causing debilitating headaches, nausea, and fatigue, leading to missed work, social isolation, and decreased quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with migraine is a blend of feverfew, ginger, and lavender. Feverfew contains parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that ease pain and reduce nausea, while lavender promotes relaxation and reduces stress, a common migraine trigger. Together, these teas create a powerful combination that not only alleviates migraine symptoms but also supports overall well-being. This blend can be brewed and consumed as needed to help manage migraines.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian Red Crescent medical journal, Coriandrum sativum L. (C. sativum) fruit syrup was found to be effective in reducing the duration, severity, and frequency of migraine attacks. In a trial involving 68 migraine patients, who were randomly assigned to either an intervention group receiving C. sativum syrup or a control group receiving a placebo, the results showed that the C. sativum syrup significantly reduced the duration, severity, and frequency of migraine attacks. Specifically, the study found that the mean migraine duration was reduced by 5.7 hours, the severity was reduced by 3.65 units, and the frequency was reduced by about 50% compared to the control group.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas helps with migraine because of its potential to relax and soothe the nervous system. Additionally, fennel tea's natural sedative effects may help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted during a migraine episode. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, fennel tea may provide relief from migraine symptoms and promote overall well-being.

This combination of properties makes ginger tea a popular natural remedy for migraine relief. Its effectiveness has been backed by traditional use and some scientific studies.

The plant's essential oils, including cis- and trans-ester and sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce inflammation and pain associated with migraines.
